title: World Oceans Day 2026: How diving is changing lives in Andaman’s Karen and Ranchi communities
content: The ocean has always fed the Karen and Ranchi communities. Now, it is helping them build careers, concrete homes and futures in a way their ancestors never imagined

title: Former Prime Minister H.D. Deve Gowda could be coming to end of stint in public office on June 25
content: Deve Gowda’s JD(S) does not have the numbers to get him re-elected to the Rajya Sabha of Parliament once his tenure comes to an end on June 25

title: Maharashtra hotel denied stay over 'Kashmiri identity': Jammu and Kashmir BJP functionary
content: Sajid Yousuf Shah, BJP's co-media in-charge for Jammu & Kashmir, took to X to narrate his experience. The hotel owner, however, denied the charge

title: Burglars decamp with cash, jewellery worth ₹1.5 crore from BRS leader Suresh Reddy’s Hyderabad residence
content: The police have also deployed special teams to analyse CCTV footage from the surrounding area to trace the movements of the suspects and determine how they gained access to the property

title: Assam CM Himanta Biswa Sarma allocates portfolios to 12 newly inducted Ministers
content: The Chief Minister retained Home and Political, PWD, Power, Information and Public Relations, and departments not allotted to any other Minister

title: SC upholds NGT order that landlord cannot be liable for environmental violations committed by tenant
content: The NGT, earlier, had held that owner could not be made to pay interim environmental damage compensation of ₹25 lakh for offences committed by the industrial unit operating on his rented premises

title: CB-CID to probe theft of hard disks from TNPDCL
content: The theft case, initially handled by the Greater Chennai Police, was transferred to the CB-CID by DGP Mahesh Kumar Aggarwal

title: Kerala actor rape case: High Court judge recuses from survivor’s plea on memory card access probe
content: Judge reasons that he served as Registrar of District Judiciary when the survivor filed administrative complaint regarding unauthorised access to memory card while in judicial custody during trial proceedings

title: Leopard mauls child to death in Uttar Pradesh's Dudhwa buffer zone
content: The leopard attacked the child, who was rushed to Khamaria Community Health Centre, where he succumbed to his injuries during treatment

title: Application window for verification and re-evaluation of answer papers remained fully functional, says CBSE
content: The system remained under continuous monitoring by dedicated cybersecurity teams throughout the operational period to prevent malicious traffic and cyber threats, says CBSE

title: BEST bus accident: One dead, three injured
content: According to preliminary information, the bus driver allegedly lost control of the vehicle and as a result, the bus hit two cars, a cab and two motorbikes

title: India issues fresh Iran travel warning, urges citizens to leave
content: In view of the latest developments in the region, the Embassy advises all Indian nationals to avoid travel to Iran and those currently in Iran to exit the country using available transport

title: INDIA bloc meeting: Opposition leaders seek unity, deliberate to redraw strategy
content: Congress chief Mallikarjun Kharge highlighted that the economic environment is extremely negative and new investments are not coming in at the pace required to generate new jobs

title: Daughter, father arrested for religious conversion in U.P.; nine booked
content: In a complaint lodged with police, medicine trader Devraj Malik alleged that his son Ayush Malik (30) was converted to Islam years ago under the pretext of marriage with Chandni

title: Kharge files nomination papers for Rajya Sabha polls from Karnataka
content: Rahul Gandhi, D.K. Shivakumar, Siddaramaiah, B.K. Hariprasad accompany Mallikarjun Kharge, who is seeking re-election

title: BJP announces candidates from Karnataka, Madhya Pradesh for Rajya Sabha polls
content: The Rajya Sabha polls for 24 seats in 10 States, including Arunachal Pradesh, Gujarat, Madhya Pradesh, Rajasthan, Manipur and Karnataka, will be held on June 18

title: YouTuber Maridhas arrested for videos defaming TVK government
content: Sources said a case was registered against him by the Chennai City Cyber Crime Police for posting videos allegedly defaming the TVK-led coalition government in Tamil Nadu

title: Heavy rain alerts continue across Kerala as monsoon remains active
content: Authorities have urged residents in landslide, mudslide and flash flood-prone areas to remain vigilant and move to safer locations if advised by officials

title: Lack of privacy, toilets, persistent stigma force girls in Odisha to miss school during menstruation
content: While 94% of the surveyed schools had separate toilets for girls, the lack of basic menstrual hygiene support systems, and non-availability of water and soap were cited as major hurdles

title: Kurnool readies 200 trainers for Yogandhra; mega yoga event on June 21
content: District Collector A. Siri leads a yoga session at Orvakal’s Rock Garden, urging residents to register as the initiative expands to tourist spots and six temples in the district.

title: Akhilesh alleges massive fraud with Ram Temple money; trust says audit underway
content: It is very sensitive news for devotees of Lord Ram across the world that crores of rupees from donations made to the Ram Temple have been found missing, says Samajwadi Party president Akhilesh Yadav

title: Experts question Kerala’s move to assess carrying capacity of forests and conduct wildlife census to mitigate conflicts
content: Environmentalists and wildlife experts argue that it risks reducing a complex ecological issue to wildlife numbers while overlooking habitat degradation, forest fragmentation and human pressures on forest ecosystems

title: Woman killed, son injured in wild elephant attack in Kerala’s Idukki
content: Forest dept officials said the woman was walking her son to school when they encountered two wild elephants standing on the road. Due to heavy rain and thick mist, she failed to notice the animals

title: Pay ₹10 lakh each to three child victims of sexual offence, orders Madras High Court
content: Justice A.D. Jagadish Chandira directs Tamil Nadu government to pay the amount from the Child Victim Compensation Fund

title: Free KSRTC bus travel for women: Only for ordinary services in first 100 days; more services later
content: Finance Secretary tasked with studying how to compensate KSRTC for revenue loss resulting from scheme implementation, says Transport Minister C.P. John

title: AICC functionary Suraj Hegde passes away in Bengaluru
content: Leader of Opposition in Lok Sabha Rahul Gandhi, AICC President Mallikarjun M. Kharge, Chief Minister D.K. Shivakumar and KPCC President B. K. Hariprasad were among the many leaders who offered their condolences

title: Three Air India aircraft parked at Delhi airport hit by ground equipment; taken out of operation
content: The airport operator said that of the three impacted aircraft, two will be back to operations soon while the third will take a little longer to be fixed

title: Narcotics trafficking: Enforcement Directorate raids multiple locations in Tripura, Mizoram, West Bengal
content: Officials said the premises that were raided are located close to the India-Myanmar International Border in Mizoram and India-Bangladesh international front in Tripura

title: After IAP exit, Hyderabad paediatrician Sivaranjani Santosh alleges pharma influence, seeks probe into ORSL promotion
content: In a post on social media platform X, Dr Santosh said that the acceptance of her resignation had freed her from any obligation to remain restrained in criticising the academy’s leadership

title: Supreme Court-appointed amicus curiae seeks direction to MCD on action taken against illegal constructions
content: Senior advocate and amicus curiae Ajit Kumar Sinha in a report on June 4, approached the top court seeking urgent intervention following the collapse of the building in Saidulajab area

title: Who was Sathyendra? The Chennai cinephile who always had questions
content: The cinephile, whose viral Leo review made headlines, passed away recently. Chennai’s film community rose to the occasion

title: INDIA bloc only a figment of imagination, lacks conviction on ground: BJP
content: The BJP national spokesperson  says that the Aam Aadmi Party has also questioned the Congress’ intentions behind “forging” alliances with regional parties

title: ‘Resigned to avoid controversy’: Sunny Joseph defends Benny Thomas appointment, says no legal issues
content: Minister says his brother-in-law himself took the initiative to step down after the appointment became a subject of political debate

title: PM Modi completes 12 years in office, says government focussed on dignity and opportunity to people
content: Modi was sworn-in as Prime Minister for the third consecutive time on June 9, 2024, following another victory in the 2024 Parliamentary elections

title: Large-scale tree plantation most effective way to tackle rising temperature: Professor S.L. Madivalar
content: He inaugurated a cleanliness drive and sapling plantation programme organised by the Parisarakkaagi Naavu Sanghatane, in association with the State Pollution Control Board, at Gandhi Shanti Pratishthana in Dharwad

title: Political tensions escalate in Kerala’s Ambalappuzha as G. Sudhakaran targets CPI(M), Pinarayi Vijayan in provocative speech
content: In his address, Sudhakaran likened Pinarayi Vijayan to a “drenched chicken” in the Assembly, implying that the former Chief Minister and current Opposition Leader appeared subdued and ineffective

title: Trinamool Congress MP Sukhendu Sekhar Roy resigns from Rajya Sabha and party
content: The resignation of Trinamool Congress MP Sukhendu Sekhar Roy brings to the fore differences among the party MPs, and speculations are rife that more MPs may follow suit

title: K. Annamalai | New chapter
content: The former BJP Tamil Nadu president has quit the party and launched a new ‘movement’, promising to bring ‘constructive’ and ‘clean’ politics

title: Muslim leaders warn Congress against ignoring their concerns in Karnataka
content: The government, they claim, had not acted on demands to enhance reservation under Category 2(B) from 4% to 8%, allocate ₹10,000 crore for minority welfare in the State Budget, and ensure proportionate representation for minorities in the Legislative Assembly and Legislative Council

title: Ahmedabad plane crash: A year on, victim’s father rebuilds life; says son kept his promise even after death
content: Girdharbhai Kalawadia, father of Mahesh Jirawala, killed in the 2025 Ahmedabad plane crash said ‘we are living in our own home’ because of his son

title: LPG price hike inevitable due to global crisis: Union Minister Pralhad Joshi
content: The domestic cooking gas LPG price has been raised by ₹29 per cylinder, marking the second increase in three months as state-owned fuel retailers continue to grapple with elevated global energy costs

title: Government hospitals’ share in organ donation rises in Tamil Nadu
content: A number of factors has contributed to this, including systematic reorientation for government doctors, standardisation of operational protocols, and multi-level periodic reviews

title: Veerappan’s claim: Actor Sukanya wins defamation case in Madras High Court after 30 years
content: Madras High Court directs Sun TV Network to pay her ₹10 lakh towards damages for not editing out scandalous allegations made against her by forest brigand Veerappan in a 1996 interview

title: Puneeth Rajkumar Hrudaya Jyothi scheme helps save nearly 16,000 cardiac patients in Karnataka
content: Officials said Artificial Intelligence (AI)-enabled technology has played a key role in facilitating rapid diagnosis

title: 108 ambulances arrive faster as average response time improves
content: In Chennai, the average response time declined from 7.45 minutes in 2023-2024 to 6.14 minutes in 2026-2027 (till April)

title: Trinamool Congress leader and Falta candidate Jahangir Khan arrested
content: Jahangir Khan had withdrawn from the contest at Falta re-poll on May 21 and has been on the run since the polling was held

title: Internet services suspended, prohibitory orders imposed in Jaipur for anti-encroachment drive
content: The demolition drive includes the removal of five religious structures encroaching within the Nandpuri road’s designated boundary

title: Tamil Nadu government rolls out online registration of select documents
content: Select documents can be registered online from anywhere round the clock. The Registration Department is planning to make this system mandatory soon

title: Bengaluru man found dead in Hassan
content: The deceased, Manjesh Kumar, faced criminal charges in multiple cases registered under the Arms Act and the SC/ST Prevention of Atrocities Act at Talaghattapura Police Station in Bengaluru

title: After murder, stayed 30 mins, changed: DU prof’s tenants killed her, brought minor son to 'blend in'
content: A Delhi University professor's murder in east Delhi has been linked to a multi-crore ancestral property in West Bengal. Police arrested a couple from Bardhaman and apprehended their minor son, alleging they killed the professor after she refused to sell her share of the inherited property.

title: OpenAI CFO: I wouldn't hire a finance person who doesn’t know how to use Excel
content: OpenAI's CFO, Sarah Friar, stressed that AI tool proficiency is now as crucial for finance professionals as spreadsheets were decades ago.  She highlighted that companies are actively seeking employees skilled in AI, with demand for compute power outstripping supply. Friar also underscored the need for inclusive access to AI technologies as adoption grows.

title: Jahangir Khan, TMC's 'Pushpa', arrested at India-Nepal border on 'extortion' charges
content: The arrest comes weeks after Calcutta high court's vacation bench refused to extend the interim protection in five FIRs granted to the TMC strongman. Justice Partha Sarathi Chatterjee referred the matter to a regular bench. Earlier, Khan was granted interim protection by Justice Saugata Bhattacharyya till May 26, considering the repolls there on May 21.

title: 'You don't need a turning track': Gavaskar decodes Suthar's six-wicket haul
content: Debutant Manav Suthar starred as India bowled out Afghanistan for 152 and enforced the follow-on in the lone Test. The left-arm spinner claimed 6 for 33, recording the second-best figures by an Indian on Test debut. Sunil Gavaskar praised Suthar’s accuracy, control and skill, saying his success came from precision and smart bowling rather than pitch assistance.

title: Should women work? Edelweiss MF CEO asks as India's birth rate falls
content: India's fertility rate has dropped below replacement level, prompting a new economic discussion.  Radhika Gupta emphasizes that with fewer births, worker productivity, skills, and female workforce participation become crucial growth drivers.  The challenge lies in making careers and family life sustainable together, requiring robust childcare and care infrastructure, akin to traditional economic development.

title: Ceasefire gave Iran breathing room: How Tehran used it to replenish its arsenal
content: Following an April 8 ceasefire, Iran has rapidly rebuilt its military might, reopening vital missile and drone storage tunnels. Despite extensive US strikes, Tehran has repaired facilities, reorganized logistics, and accelerated production.  With a significant portion of its arsenal intact and drone output high, Iran is leveraging this period to bolster its capabilities and influence regional dynamics.

title: 'It's called 'idli', not 'id-slice': Tharoor dishes out another culinary lesson
content: Congress leader Shashi Tharoor amused netizens with his witty responses to culinary debates on social media. He humorously critiqued an 'idli pizza' cutting style, attributing it to software engineering logic. Previously, Tharoor also defended the South Indian idli against a comparison to rasgulla, highlighting its unique preparation and culinary significance.

title: Cognizant's AI Builder strategy opens roles to non-tech candidates
content: Cognizant CEO Ravi Kumar S. believes fears of widespread job losses due to AI are overstated, predicting more jobs will emerge. He highlighted Cognizant's new AI Builder strategy, creating roles like Frontier Certified Engineer and Frontier Business Operator that don't require technical backgrounds.

title: Watch: CM Vijay plays chess with Praggnanandhaa after announcing Rs 50 lakh reward
content: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay honoured chess prodigy R. Praggnanandhaa with Rs 50 lakh and a memento after his historic Norway Chess triumph. The 20-year-old became the first Indian to win the title, defeating Magnus Carlsen twice and D. Gukesh. After starting poorly, he staged a remarkable comeback, winning four straight games to secure the championship.

title: Affordable housing now crosses Rs 45 lakh — but govt limit stays unchanged
content: India's urban areas face a growing affordable housing crisis, with the deficit projected to reach 30 million units by 2030. Developers are shifting focus away from affordable homes due to land scarcity and financial viability challenges, leading to a significant drop in new affordable housing launches.

title: 'Every red light was making my heart race': 61 km, two exams, and a father’s X post
content: A post on X by Shailendra Sharma has drawn attention to the logistical strain faced by students during competitive exams. He described rushing his daughter between two national-level tests, IISER and CUET, across 61 kilometres in Delhi-NCR within hours. The incident raises concerns about exam scheduling coordination and student convenience.

title: How Israel's air-defence network intercepts everything from ballistic missiles to drones
content: Amid rising tensions, Iran launched a series of eleven missiles aimed at Israel, yet each was thwarted by the country's intricate air defense system. This episode not only reveals the prowess of Israel's military technology but also its readiness to combat any form of aerial assaults, whether from makeshift devices or sophisticated missile strikes.

title: CBSE revaluation row: Delhi HC issues notice to board, Centre on OSM discrepancy plea
content: The Delhi High Court has issued notices to the Centre and CBSE regarding discrepancies in the board's On-Screen Marking system. This follows student allegations of handwriting mismatches in scanned answer sheets, raising concerns about marking irregularities. The court will hear the case on June 12.

title: Shaurya Shikhar couldn't crack IIT-JEE; at 26, he surprised father with Rs 55 lakh BMW
content: Ankur Warikoo's viral LinkedIn post highlights Shaurya Shikhar's journey from failing IIT-JEE and feeling inadequate to earning a seven-figure monthly income and gifting his father a ₹55 lakh BMW. The story emphasizes that life eventually works out, urging readers to keep moving forward through difficult times, a message resonating widely online.

title: 4 bullets for wife, 7 for son: Gurgaon security officer held for double murder
content: A shocking incident unfolded in Gurgaon's Ashok Vihar Phase 3 where a security officer allegedly shot his wife and son dead following a heated family argument. The 56-year-old man reportedly used his licensed revolver after his son intervened in a dispute between his parents. Police have taken the accused into custody.

title: CBSE clarifies 'roll number not found' issue after handling 3.8 lakh answer book requests
content: The CBSE announced that over 1.6 lakh students successfully submitted applications through its verification and re-evaluation portal between June 2 and June 7, 2026, covering more than 3.8 lakh answer books. The process followed concerns over the board’s new On-Screen Marking (OSM) system and was conducted under the supervision of government agencies and IIT experts. CBSE said the portal remained operational despite cyber threats and clarified that the “Roll Number Not Found” message applied only to ineligible candidates.

title: ‘He deserves his place’: De Villiers backs selectors' bold call on Sooryavanshi
content: South African legend AB de Villiers lauded India's decision to select 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi for international duty, calling it a "brave" and "right call." De Villiers believes Sooryavanshi possesses the talent to succeed across formats, though he acknowledged the unique challenges of Test cricket. The young prodigy's selection follows a dominant performance in IPL 2026.

title: 'He knows I helped him': Gehlot on Sachin Pilot, 2022 Rajasthan crisis & a 'big conspiracy'
content: Former Rajasthan CM Ashok Gehlot expressed hurt over Sachin Pilot's alleged failure to publicly acknowledge his role in becoming a Union minister. Gehlot also claimed a "big conspiracy" unfairly damaged his reputation during the 2022 Congress presidential race, leading to a revolt by his loyal MLAs.

title: 100 days of Middle East crisis: What happens when the Strait of Hormuz opens
content: Iran has indicated the Strait of Hormuz will reopen under new conditions set by Iranian and Omani authorities, potentially including transit fees for services. This comes as oil flows remain constrained following the US-Israeli conflict.

title: Saleem Safi walks off without review as animated scenes unfold near Afghan dugout
content: India tightened their grip on the one-off Test against Afghanistan, enforcing the follow-on after a dominant first innings. The morning session was marked by a significant DRS blunder from Afghanistan, where a batter walked off without a review, only for replays to show the decision would have been overturned. Debutant Manav Suthar impressed with six wickets.

title: Modern-day Brahmastra? How hypersonic missiles could change the rules of war
content: India is rapidly advancing in hypersonic weapon technology, achieving a significant scramjet breakthrough. This development positions the nation among global leaders like the US, China, and Russia, who are all racing to develop these near-unstoppable weapons. Hypersonic systems, flying above Mach 5, pose a new era of warfare, demanding advanced defense strategies.

title: 'Rahul lacks consistency': Anti-Congress posters surface as INDIA bloc meets
content: Posters targeting the Leader of Opposition in Lok Sabha, Rahul Gandhi, appeared on multiple roundabouts in New Delhi ahead of the INDIA bloc meeting on Monday. The posters highlighted past statements by Opposition leaders against the Congress MP.

title: Messi GOAT tour fiasco: Ex-minister Aroop Biswas' urgent hearing plea rejected by Calcutta HC
content: The Calcutta High Court refused to urgently hear a petition by former minister Aroop Biswas seeking to quash an FIR against him for criminal intimidation. The court directed Biswas to follow the regular legal process, suggesting he seek anticipatory bail instead, as the FIR was filed six months after the alleged incident.

title: Watch: Earthquake in Philippines leaves 15 dead, 200 injured, tsunami warning in many countries
content: A powerful 8.1-magnitude earthquake struck the Philippines, causing buildings to collapse and triggering tsunami warnings. High school and university structures were among those damaged, with viral videos showing crumbling buildings. Authorities issued tsunami warnings for the Philippines and neighboring Indonesia, with potential waves exceeding one meter.

title: Four walls, soaring costs: Why is your housing dream getting costlier
content: Housing affordability has plunged globally, hitting young people and vulnerable groups hardest. Soaring property prices and rising rents outpace incomes, with India's major cities facing a severe crunch. Experts cite urbanization and supply shortages. Governments must integrate housing with other strategies and boost construction to address this growing crisis.

title: Suthar scripts history on debut, registers best bowling figures in 38 years
content: Manav Suthar enjoyed a dream Test debut against Afghanistan, claiming 6/33. The 23-year-old registered the best bowling figures in an innings on Test debut by an Indian in 38 years. It is the best since Narendra Hirwani's 8/61 against West Indies in 1988. The left-arm spinner helped bowl Afghanistan out for 152 and joined an elite list featuring Hirwani, R Ashwin, Dilip Doshi and Syed Abid Ali.

title: TMC neta accused of graft tonsured, paraded with garland of shoes by villagers, arrested
content: A Trinamool leader accused of extensive corruption, including embezzlement and land grabbing, was publicly humiliated by villagers in Howrah. Residents shaved his head, garbed him in a shoe garland, and paraded him before handing him over to the police, who have since arrested him.

title: Rare visit: China's Xi Jinping meets North Korea's Kim Jong Un in Pyongyang
content: Chinese President Xi Jinping began a state visit to North Korea, meeting with leader Kim Jong Un to strengthen strategic ties. Xi emphasized their "unbreakable and enduring" friendship and commitment to regional peace. The visit occurs amid heightened tensions and North Korea's non-negotiable stance on its nuclear program.

title: Ex-principal’s passion takes root, barren Madhya Pradesh hill turns plantation
content: Inspired by Assam's "Forest Man," retired principal SL Garg transformed a barren Madhya Pradesh hillock into a thriving 50,000-tree plantation over a decade.  Keshar Parvat now boasts over 600 plant species, including exotic fruits and saffron, demonstrating successful ecological restoration and the potential for widespread greening.

title: ‘I don’t have to change’: Shreyas embraces India captaincy challenge in his own way
content: Shreyas Iyer embraces his new role as India's T20I captain, vowing to remain true to his personality and competitive Mumbai upbringing.  He believes his inherent winning mindset will guide him, seeing the captaincy as a significant challenge.  This appointment marks a strong comeback for Iyer, who now leads the team after a period out of T20I plans.

title: Akshay Kumar on why he does not take credit when he gives back to community
content: Akshay Kumar revealed on 'Aap Ki Adaalat' why he prefers not to publicize his extensive charitable contributions, amounting to over ₹76 crore for various causes. He believes it's a privilege to serve and would feel foolish boasting about it. Kumar also addressed being called 'money-minded,' highlighting his hard work and status as a top taxpayer.

title: Watch: Indigo stepladder crashes into parked Air India aircraft at Delhi airport
content: Strong winds and rain at Delhi airport caused a passenger stepladder to roll into a parked Air India aircraft. The sudden weather event also blew ground support equipment into three other Air India planes, damaging them and forcing them out of service. No injuries were reported during the chaotic incident.

title: Bibi defies Trump's call for restraint; Israel & Iran trade blows: How the sudden flare-up unfolded
content: The strikes could also derailed ​​United States presdient Donald Trump's last-ditch effort to find an off ramp in the war by negotiating a nuclear deal with Iran. Trump, who had been breathing down the Netanyahu's neck to restrain from hostilites, in a recent interview spoke on how he "calls the shot", affirming that Netanyahu must accept the Iran deal to put cessation to the bloody war.

title: Woman gym trainer, father accused of converting UP bizman's son to Islam
content: Uttar Pradesh Police apprehended two individuals in Shamli for a forced religious conversion plot involving emotional entrapment and blackmail for financial gain. This incident is part of a larger crackdown, with 16 arrests made across the district in various cases, including narcotics, theft, and attempted murder. Authorities are committed to combating illegal activities and urge public cooperation.

title: 'Why are you asking this question?': Harmanpreet puts reporter in awkward spot - Watch
content: Ahead of the Women's T20 World Cup in England, Indian captain Harmanpreet Kaur shut down a reporter asking if this was her final tournament, bluntly counter-questioning if she should retire. India is placed in a demanding Group A alongside heavyweights like Australia and South Africa, and begins its campaign against arch-rivals Pakistan on June 14.

title: Son caught cheating, cop ‘vandalises’ Dehradun college office, threatens teachers
content: A police sub-inspector and four associates allegedly stormed a Dehradun polytechnic college, vandalizing property and threatening staff after his son was caught cheating. CCTV footage captured both the cheating incident and the subsequent alleged assault. Both parties have filed FIRs, and the SI has been suspended pending investigation.

title: Trump Says Iran Agreed To No Nuclear Weapons, Experts Not Convinced
content: According to the White House, Trump has said at least 74 times since 2011 that Iran must never obtain a nuclear weapon.

title: Mamata Banerjee Attends INDIA Meet Amid A Parallel Trinamool MPs' Huddle
content: Sources told NDTV that a senior Trinamool MP, who had to travel from Mumbai to Kolkata last evening, had an unplanned layover in Delhi.

title: YouTuber Gives Home Tour, Shows Gold, Gets Robbed Of Items Worth Rs 10 Lakh
content: The burglars took away gold, silver jewellery, cash and a carton of energy drink, worth around Rs 8 lakh to Rs 10 lakh, the YouTuber said.

title: Telangana Leader K Kavitha's "Taliban" Jab At Chief Minister Revanth Reddy
content: K Kavitha took a swipe at Chief Minister Revanth Reddy over his government's decision to bring down the school count in the state from 27,000 to 4,000.

title: Trinamool Rajya Sabha MP Sukhendu Sekhar Ray Quits Amid Spiralling Crisis
content: Setback For Mamata Banerjee- Trinamool Rajya Sabha MP Sukhendu Sekhar Roy Quits Party Amid Spiralling Crisis

title: Why One Of Finance's Most Powerful Leaders Says AI Left Him "Depressed"
content: Citadel Founder and CEO Ken Griffin is widely considered one of the most powerful and influential leaders in modern finance. The boss of the most profitable hedge fund in history recently revealed AI left him depressed.

title: Stock Market LIVE Updates, Sensex Today: Sensex Sheds 600 Points Amid Fresh Tensions In Middle East
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Oil prices rise more than $2 after Israel launched renewed strikes.

title: Lebanon's Crusader Castle Seized By Israel A Symbol Of Bloody History
content: When Israeli forces entered the 900-year-old castle on Saturday, they were returning to a fortress they held throughout their 1982-2000 presence in southern Lebanon.

title: INDIA Bloc Meet Live Updates: Congress Calls For "Spirit Of Unity", DMK, AAP Skip Meeting
content: INDIA Bloc Meet Live Updates: 23 political parties of the INDIA bloc are likely to meet after key partners TMC and DMK faced defeat in the recently concluded Assembly polls.

title: Pre-Marital Physical Relationship Not A Mark On Character: Supreme Court
content: The case stemmed from allegations of sex on the false promise of marriage involving a woman known to the candidate. The matter was later settled before a Lok Adalat in 2015.

title: On CCTV, 1 Dead As Bus Rams Vehicles In Mumbai During Peak Traffic Hour
content: The bus lost control and rammed a two-wheeler, an electric bike, an electric car, a taxi and a Baleno car.

title: When Will Monsoon Reach Delhi? Weather Office Shares Update
content: The southwest monsoon arrived in Kerala on June 4, three days behind schedule, and is now expected to reach Delhi and NCR between June 25 and June 30

title: 'Bonds Are Boring': 5 Myths Investors Need To Stop Believing
content: As market volatility keeps investors on edge, bonds are quietly emerging as a crucial tool for balancing risk and building more resilient portfolios.

title: Man Shoots Wife, 25-Year-old Son Dead At Home In Gurugram
content: According to the police, the couple frequently quarrelled and had an argument late on Saturday night.

title: Madhya Pradesh Rajya Sabha Race Intensifies As BJP Eyes Third Seat
content: Earlier, senior BJP leader Kailash Vijayvargiya had indicated that if the party fielded a third candidate, it would work to ensure his victory. That statement now appears less like speculation and more like strategy.

title: Khalistanis flags, Indira Gandhi assassination tableau on display at Sikh parade in Canada
content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. With an unwavering commitment to accuracy, depth, and timeliness, we strive to keep you informed about the ever-evolving world, delivering a nuanced understanding of international affairs to our readers. Join us on a journey across continents as we unravel the stories that shape our interconnected world. Read More

title: New policy redrawing curricula: French, German find themselves out of class
content: Meghna Dhulia is an Assistant News Editor at The Times of India, bringing over a decade of experience in journalism. She specialises in education reporting, covering both foundational and higher education with a clear, insightful lens. Her work sheds light on critical issues shaping the future—ranging from policy impact and systemic challenges to compelling human stories from the field. Her experience also extends to the news desk, where she has overseen editorial workflows, ensuring readers receive balanced, reliable and impactful news. Read More

title: Hidden victims: Air pollution may harm babies before birth
content: Anuja Jaiswal is a Senior Assistant Editor at The Times of India, with an impressive 18-year career in narrative journalism. title: No phone, internet, housed at secret location: NEET paper setters to be in lockdown till re-exam
content: Manash Pratim Gohain is a seasoned journalist with over two decades at The Times of India, where he has built a rich body of work spanning education policy, politics, and governance. Renowned for his incisive coverage of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, accreditation reforms, and skilling initiatives, he has also reported on student politics, urban policy, and social movements. His political reportage—both reflective and news-driven—adds depth to his writing, bridging policy with public impact. Through his 2,500 articles and related outlets, he has emerged as a trusted voice in national discourse, particularly in linking education reform to broader societal change. Read More

title: World comes to China, Xi goes to North Korea: Why Kim Jong Un makes Beijing uneasy
